source_note: Derived from Le Cuong Nguyen's pre-AI cover letters, product/gamification notes, and early authentic blog articles written between 2012 and 2019. Use as a voice-DNA reference, not as a structure or grammar template.

preserve_patterns: start from a real reason for writing
show conviction through contribution rather than status
sound like a real person who noticed something specific about the role
avoid_patterns: repeating the same confidence line in every paragraph
grammar or phrasing quirks from old letters
preferred_opening_moves: state why the role caught attention
connect the role to one real strength area
preferred_motivation_moves: explain why the company or environment is interesting in human terms
tie the role to operations, workflows, users, or systems when relevant
preferred_closing_moves: invite discussion or interview without stiffness
secondary_signals: authority should come from lived experience and what was learned
short lesson-like lines are acceptable when they stay formal and restrained
show scars and growth without sounding dramatic

core_traits: playful when the topic allows it
mentor-like and battle-tested when the topic is career or product work
preserve_patterns: keep open questions visible when they add realism
move naturally between concept, mechanism, and human behavior
turn painful experience into practical lessons
use short takeaway lines or mini slogans when they sharpen the lesson
avoid_patterns: turning everything into polished framework language
erasing the exploratory feel of early drafts

signature_patterns: advice grounded in incidents rather than theory
